text.fullText:
Personally, on one hand, I wouldn't be opposed to knowing what the broker is making on the load. But, I'm a realist that believes that everyone involved needs to make money. If I agree to haul for a certain number, that's on me. Although, a little more transparency might help the negotiation process.
On the other hand, in this free market society we live in, you'll always have both carriers & brokers that each think the other is making too much on the load. This may cause a problem with overzealous brokers or carriers. The brokers will always attempt to make the most they can, understandably. As well as the carrier, this is capitalism. I am a carrier, we don't use brokers a lot, but I look to make 25% over my expenses. The cost of everything has gone up substantially in the last few years, and we still need to budget for equipment replacement. Sometimes, 25% works out. Sometimes it doesn't. At the end of the day, I think it would be a good thing to have more transparency between brokers and carriers, for realists like me. Maybe not for carriers thinking of limiting the amount a broker can make, you agreed to the haul rate with the broker, which means it's good for your business in one way or another. If the rate negotiation with the broker can't get you where you need to be, look for something else.
